mnemonics: F-Strings Are Super Fast (FASF): Remember that f-strings are the modern, fastest, and most readable way to format strings in Python.
keyPoints: Use f-strings (f'...') for modern and readable string formatting.
The .format() method is versatile and useful for more complex scenarios.
The old %-formatting is considered legacy and should be avoided in new code.
You can directly include variables and expressions inside f-strings within curly braces {}.
Format specifications (like :.2f for numbers) can be added inside the curly braces for precise control.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the 'f' prefix before an f-string literal.
Using the + operator for complex string concatenation instead of f-strings, which is less efficient and readable.
Using the wrong type of quotation mark inside the f-string, causing a syntax error.
Trying to use a variable name inside an f-string without wrapping it in curly braces {}.
